beats.title: Leaving Karawa
beats.boundaryReason: Opening departure scene from Karawa through the first major trail analysis at the creature split site.
dateEvidence: The session opens the next morning after the attack on Karawa.
The party departs in pre-dawn light and reaches the track junction by midday or early afternoon.

beats.title: Choosing the Route
beats.boundaryReason: The party shifts from trail interpretation into route planning, first-night camp discussion, and the uneventful next day of northbound travel.
dateEvidence: The party decides to ride straight for Raven's Hold and makes camp after the first day.
A later DM transition marks the next day of travel as uneventful.

beats.title: Meeting Shani
beats.boundaryReason: A new travel day begins at the road crossing and centers on scouting, meeting Shani's refugee band, and learning about the storm and gnolls.
dateEvidence: The beat begins with 'The next day' as the party approaches the road.
Shani describes the previous night's gnoll attack, placing the encounter on the third day of travel.

beats.title: Ambush at Camp
beats.boundaryReason: The party settles into camp and the gnoll ambush erupts, covering the opening rounds and the first scramble for cover.
dateEvidence: The party makes camp after leaving Shani's band.
The fight interrupts their rest during the night.

beats.title: Trading Arrows
beats.boundaryReason: The ambush settles into a ranged exchange as the party uses rocks, magic, and the floating rabbit distraction to survive the middle of the fight.
dateEvidence: This beat continues the same overnight combat.
The party is still under arrow fire and has not yet ended the encounter.

beats.title: Breaking the Gnolls
beats.boundaryReason: The final combat phase turns in the party's favor as the wounded gnolls are finished off and the remaining attackers break.
dateEvidence: The combat has carried long enough to bridge into the next morning.
This phase ends when only the cleanup and recovery remain.

beats.title: Vulture Warnings
beats.boundaryReason: After the fight the party regroups, tends to recovery, and Seeker questions a vulture about movement near the fortress and storm.
dateEvidence: The battle is over and the party is packing camp later that morning.
The vulture scene happens after dawn with thermals rising.

beats.title: Across the Silent Plains
beats.boundaryReason: After the vulture exchange, the party resumes northbound travel over two quiet days until they camp near the mountains.
dateEvidence: The party departs after the morning vulture scene.
A later DM transition marks the next day as another hot, quiet ride near the mountains.

beats.title: Centaurs at Dawn
beats.boundaryReason: The party camps south of the mountains, senses the tainted land at dusk, and wakes to the sudden arrival of a centaur band.
dateEvidence: The beat begins as the party sets camp for the night near the pass.
The next morning they are surprised by fast-moving riders from the east.

beats.title: Parley with Tharandros
beats.boundaryReason: The final scene is the long centaur parley, ending when Tharandros agrees to ride south and the party halts short of Raven's Hold.
dateEvidence: The centaur conversation follows immediately after the dawn encounter.
The session ends after the centaurs depart south and the party turns toward Raven's Hold.
